headline: Smurfit Westrock Capacity Cuts And Price Hikes Test Earnings Resilience
summary: Smurfit Westrock (NYSE:SW) is closing nearly 600,000 tons of paper capacity as part of a new round of capacity rationalizations. The company is implementing a second wave of containerboard price increases across the industry in an inflationary cost backdrop. Synergy capture from recent actions is currently running ahead of internal expectations. Smurfit Westrock, trading at $39.51, is reshaping its operations with sizable capacity cuts that aim to support cost efficiency and pricing...
